note: Logical entity model for green-ai. No DB schema. No classes. No API endpoints. Fields are logical — not DB column types.

aggregates.name: Messaging
aggregates.description: Root aggregate for broadcast lifecycle, recipient management, and message delivery. Owns SmsGroup as aggregate root. StatusProgressionGuard applies to all status writes (architect decision 2026-04-13).

entities.name: Broadcast
entities.description: Root aggregate entity. A named broadcast campaign covering one or more channels (SMS, Email, Voice, Eboks). Owns its lifecycle state machine (DRAFT → ACTIVATED → LOOKUP → SENT). Created under a Profile within a Customer.
fields: lookupConfig (LookupConfig value object: lookupPrivate, lookupBusiness, sendToOwner, sendToAddress, ownerAddressType)
invariants: BR001 — active=true blocks all modifications
BR002 — isLookedUp=true blocks re-compose after activation
BR004 — activate is idempotent when already active
BR005 — activating with zero recipients raises NoRecipientsException
BR006 — activating with >25000 Kvhx triggers large-broadcast warning
BR007 / BR028 — scheduledSendAt set always routes to batch lookup path (IsWebLookupNeeded=false)
BR008 — web lookup gate: ByAddress + fromApi=true + ≤5 items + no zip-only
R037 — lastMinuteLookup=true AND scheduledSendAt set → skip lookup at activate; ScheduledLookupTrigger fires 5 min before send
legacy_field_notes: SmsLog.Email field marked ToDo:Remove — do NOT carry forward to green-ai
GatewayId / ExternalRefId marked ToDo:InUse?? — verify before including

entities.name: BroadcastSmsContent
entities.description: SMS-specific content for a broadcast. Extension entity: SmsGroupId is sole PK/FK. 1:1 with Broadcast.
fields: sendAs (string — sender name or phone number, max 11 chars — BR017)
invariants: BR017 — sendAs truncated to 11 chars at dispatch
BR015 — messageText required when broadcast.channels includes SMS

entities.name: BroadcastEmailContent
entities.description: Email-specific content for a broadcast. Extension entity: SmsGroupId is sole PK/FK. 1:1 with Broadcast.
fields: sendAs (string — sender email, max 100 chars — BR018)
invariants: BR016 — subject without body is invalid
BR018 — sendAs max 100 chars

entities.name: RecipientCriterion
entities.description: One recipient specification within a broadcast. Defines an address range, direct phone/email, or a StandardReceiver reference. Used as input to the address lookup pipeline. StatusCode tracks per-criterion lookup outcome.
fields: address (AddressCriterion value object: street, zip, city, fromNumber, toNumber, letter, floor, door, evenOdd, meters)
invariants: At least one of: phone, address criterion, or standardReceiverId must be set
BR005 — broadcast must have at least one RecipientCriterion with resolvable recipients before activation
relationships: OPTIONAL-REF StandardReceiver (AGG-SUB — read-only reference at lookup time)

entities.name: Message
entities.description: A single resolved message record. Created during address lookup from a RecipientCriterion. Carries the resolved phone number, merge-field-resolved text, and current delivery status. StatusProgressionGuard enforces monotonic status transitions.
invariants: R035 + StatusProgressionGuard — statusCode may only increase in rank. Lower-ranked callbacks are rejected.
BR017 — smsSendAs max 11 chars (enforced at Message creation from BroadcastSmsContent.sendAs)
Terminal statuses (DELIVERED, FAILED_FINAL) cannot be overwritten
legacy_notes: Email field on SmsLog marked ToDo:Remove — NOT included in Message entity. Use emailSendAs/emailSubject fields already present.

entities.name: DeliveryStatusEvent
entities.description: An individual gateway DLR (delivery receipt) event for a Message. Multiple events per Message (one per gateway callback). Created by DLR callback handlers (B014/B015). Imported=false on creation, true after StatusProgressionGuard processing (B016). CROSS-DOMAIN WRITE SURFACE (SC002): Email, Voice, Eboks channels also write here via IDeliveryStatusWriter.
fields: imported (bool — false=pending reconciliation, true=processed by StatusProgressionGuard)
invariants: R036 — all channel writers (SMS, Email, Voice, Eboks) must write via IDeliveryStatusWriter
StatusProgressionGuard processes batches of 300 (B016) — imported transitions to true after processing
B014 CONFLICTING — GatewayApi DLR parses MessageId as int (truncates IDs > 2.1B). Green-ai MUST use long parse.
entities.shared_write_contract: IDeliveryStatusWriter (all channels must use this — not direct table access)

entities.name: UnresolvedMessageRecord
entities.description: A bulk record for recipients where address lookup found no phone number. Created in batches (B018). Represents no-phone outcome — not a deliverable message. Used for reporting/exclusion tracking.
fields: kvhx (string — address that had no phone)
invariants: Created only when address lookup finds no phone — not mutated after creation

entities.name: BroadcastSchedule
entities.description: Recurrence configuration for a scheduled broadcast. Contains JSON-blob interval config (not RRULE). Exception dates. Green-ai must decide: keep JSON or normalize to explicit recurrence fields.
invariants: BR028 — scheduled broadcast (scheduledSendAt set) always routes to batch lookup. No web path for scheduled.
entities.open_question: intervalConfig JSON blob — green-ai should consider normalizing to explicit recurrence model

entities.name: BroadcastAttachment
entities.description: File attachment for a broadcast (Email/Eboks channels). Blob reference.

entities.name: WebNotification
entities.description: In-app notification entity published alongside a broadcast (e.g., critical alert). Has its own FSM (Status int). EditedWebMessageId self-ref: editing creates a new draft version. Optional link to a Broadcast.
invariants: editedFromId creates version chain — editing creates new draft, not in-place mutation

aggregates.name: Customer
aggregates.description: Three-tier identity and access model. Customer (legal entity) → Profile (usage rights) → User (person). Role-based feature gating evaluated at broadcast create and activate time.

entities.name: Customer
entities.description: Root aggregate entity. Legal entity. Owns Profiles. Conversion target for Pipeline prospects.

entities.name: CustomerAccount
entities.description: Financial/billing details for a Customer. Separate entity from Customer identity. Also exists as ProspectCustomerAccount for pre-conversion configuration.

entities.name: Profile
entities.description: Usage-rights entity within a Customer. A user can hold multiple profiles across customers. Profile determines which features and broadcasts a user may create and send.

entities.name: ProfileRoleAssignment
entities.description: Assignment of a named role to a Profile. Evaluated at broadcast create and activate time to gate features. NOT enforced at DB level — application gate only.
fields: roleName (ProfileRole — enum: AlwaysOwner, CanSpecifyLookup, CanSelectLookupBusinessOrPrivate, DontSendEmail, CanSelectStdReceivers, CanSendByOnlyStdReceivers, BroadcastNoReciept, HasBenchmark, HasInfoPortal, ...)
invariants: BR023 — BroadcastNoReciept: receipt email suppressed
BR024 — AlwaysOwner: forces sendToOwner=true on broadcast create
BR025 — DontSendEmail: forces sendEmail=false on broadcast create
BR026 — CanSpecifyLookup: gates sendToOwner and sendToAddress fields
BR027 — CanSelectLookupBusinessOrPrivate: gates lookupBusiness and lookupPrivate fields

entities.name: ProfileUserMapping
entities.description: Links a User to a Profile. A user may be linked to multiple profiles. Provides the access context for a user session.

entities.name: CustomerApiKey
entities.description: API access credential scoped to a Customer. Permissions: ApiSendSingle, ApiHandleRecipients, ApiHandleMessageTemplates. Used by external systems to authenticate before calling messaging endpoints.
fields: keyHash (string — hashed, not stored in plaintext)
invariants: API key must be hashed at rest — NEVER store plaintext

entities.name: CustomerFtpSettings
entities.description: FTP access configuration for a Customer. Used for file-based data exchange (import/export).
invariants: FTP password must be hashed at rest

entities.name: CustomerNote
entities.description: Freeform notes and audit log entries against a Customer. CustomerLog is system-generated (audit trail); CustomerNote is user-authored.
invariants: AuditLog entries are immutable once created

aggregates.name: Subscription
aggregates.description: Customer-defined receiver lists (subscriptions) that can be used as recipients in broadcasts. Pricing per country/receiver combination.

entities.name: StandardReceiver
entities.description: Root entity. A pre-defined subscription list under a Customer. Can be linked to a Profile. Recipients in a broadcast can reference a StandardReceiver as a RecipientCriterion source.

entities.name: StandardReceiverGroup
entities.description: Grouping of StandardReceivers. Supports hierarchical grouping (group-of-groups). A group can have keywords for SMS opt-in.

entities.name: StandardReceiverGroupKeyword
entities.description: SMS keyword that triggers subscription opt-in to a StandardReceiverGroup.
invariants: Keyword must be unique per country

entities.name: DistributionPhone
entities.description: Phone number that receives delivery statistics reports for a StandardReceiverGroup.

entities.name: ProfileStandardReceiverMapping
entities.description: Controls which Profiles can use a StandardReceiver (or group) as a recipient source in broadcasts.
invariants: Exactly one of standardReceiverId or standardReceiverGroupId must be set

entities.name: ReceiverPriceSetting
entities.description: Price configuration per (Customer, StandardReceiver, Country). CustomerStandardReceiverSettings holds the customer-specific override. Statistics stored at RecipientCriterion level (SmsGroupItem), not per message.

aggregates.name: Import
aggregates.description: Two-phase import pipeline with crash-safe lifecycle, size-based routing, and idempotent resume behavior. R041 + R042 govern state machine and execution path.

entities.name: ImportJob
entities.description: Root entity. Represents one import operation. Owns the state machine: Queued(1)→ProcessingColumns(2)→ColumnsReady(3)→ReadyForImport(4)→DataImporting(5)→DataImported(6). Error state: -1. Sub-state ConvertingFile: 20. CommitStarted(107) is crash-resume marker written atomically before data write.
fields: status (ImportStatus — enum: Queued=1, ProcessingColumns=2, ColumnsReady=3, ReadyForImport=4, DataImporting=5, DataImported=6, CommitStarted=107, ConvertingFile=20, Error=-1)
invariants: R041 — CommitStarted(107) written BEFORE data write. On restart: if status=CommitStarted, resume. Terminal: DataImported(6) or Error(-1) reject re-run.
R042 — sizeKb < 10 → run confirm inline; sizeKb >= 10 → dispatch to Azure Batch (CreateConfirmDataImportAzureTaskAsync)
IdempotentResumeBehavior: CommitStarted enables crash recovery without double-commit

entities.name: ImportColumnMapping
entities.description: Column-to-field mapping definition for an ImportJob. Defined during Phase 1 (ImportPrepare).

entities.name: ImportRow
entities.description: A single validated data row in an ImportJob. Created during Phase 1 validation. Committed to domain in Phase 2.
invariants: Invalid rows are not committed to domain — Phase 2 only commits valid rows

entities.name: ImportSavedConfiguration
entities.description: Reusable column-mapping template for recurring import formats. Scoped to Customer, optionally to ImportPurpose.

aggregates.name: Address
aggregates.description: Per-country address resolution infrastructure. One domain, per-country adapters. Core only uses canonical Kvhx string. R043: IAddressAdapter per country. FI adapter is deferred (source closed Feb 2025).

entities.name: ResolvedAddress
entities.description: Core address entity. Canonical Kvhx string is the primary key — NOT a numeric surrogate (architect decision iteration 10). DK-source. Covers street-level detail, geography, and owner.
invariants: R023 — Kvhx string is canonical address identity. Never replaced by numeric surrogate at design level.
R043 — Core never receives country-native structures. Only Kvhx crosses adapter boundary.

entities.name: AddressOwner
entities.description: Property owner resolved from a Kvhx address. DK: Datafordeleren (two staging variants for import). Change tracking via AddressKvhxChange. Used for SendToOwner behavior in broadcasts.

entities.name: AddressKvhxChange
entities.description: Tracks Kvhx rename events (address identity changes). When a Kvhx is renumbered, all dependent records (SmsLogs, owners) need Kvhx update.

entities.name: AddressCorrection
entities.description: Manual correction record for a Kvhx. Allows operations team to override address data without touching the canonical import.
invariants: Corrections do not modify canonical ResolvedAddress — applied as an overlay

entities.name: NorwegianAddress
entities.maps_from: AddressNorwegianProperty + AddressNorwegianPropertyAddress + AddressImportNorwegianProperty + AddressImportNorwegianBuilding + AddressImportNorwegianAddress + AddressImportNorwegianStreet + AddressImportNorwegianZipCode + AddressImportNorwegianOwner + AddressImportNorwegianPerson + AddressImportNorwegianPlot + AddressImportNorwegianPlotProperty + AddressImportNorwegianDoor + AddressImportNorwegianChange
entities.description: Norwegian address entities. Kartverket source. Native key: 5-tuple (municipalityCode + farmNr + useNr + tenantNr + sectionNr). Resolved to Kvhx string at adapter boundary. Import staging tables (AddressImportNorwegian*) are adapter-internal. Operational tables (AddressNorwegianProperty, AddressNorwegianPropertyAddress) are the cache.
invariants: R043 — 5-tuple key never crosses adapter boundary. Only resolvedKvhx is exposed to core domain.
entities.note: All AddressImportNorwegian* staging tables are adapter-internal — NOT part of green-ai domain model. They exist only during import pipeline execution.

entities.name: SwedishAddress
entities.description: Swedish address entity. Lantmäteriet source. Resolves to Kvhx.
invariants: R043 — native key stays within SE adapter

entities.name: FinnishAddressImportData
entities.description: DEFERRED: AvoinData source closed Feb 2025. Staging tables exist in sms-service but are non-functional. Green-ai: represent as deferred adapter boundary — do not implement until replacement source is identified.
fields: (deferred — structure exists in DB, source unavailable)
invariants: R043 — Finnish adapter follows same pattern. Deferred until AvoinData replacement identified.

entities.name: AddressGeography
entities.maps_from: AddressGeography + AddressStreet + Municipality + Country + AddressBfeToLocalId + AddressVirtualMarking + AddressExportNorwegianStatistics
entities.description: Geographic reference data supporting address resolution. Municipality codes, country references, BFE-to-local mappings, virtual address markings, and export statistics for Norwegian data.

aggregates.name: Pipeline
aggregates.description: Sales prospect management and customer conversion. Conversion lifecycle has verified gaps (no task migration, no role activation) that are modelled as explicit constraints (R039, R040).

entities.name: Prospect
entities.description: Root aggregate entity. Pre-sales entity in conversion pipeline. Linked to a Customer after conversion (ProspectToCustomerConversion two-step). Tasks and role pre-configurations REMAIN on Prospect after conversion (architect decision: LEAVE ON PROSPECT).
fields: customerId (CustomerId? — FK to AGG-CUST, set at conversion step 1)
invariants: R038 — Conversion is two-step: PATCH 1 sets customerId + copies ContactPersons. PATCH 2 replaces CustomerProducts. NOT atomic.
R039 — ProcessTasks are NOT transferred at conversion. LEAVE ON PROSPECT. Customer starts with no inherited tasks.
R040 — ProspectUserRoleMappings are NOT applied to Customer at conversion. Role pre-config stays on Prospect as historical record.

entities.name: ProspectContactPerson
entities.description: Contact person on a Prospect. At conversion step 1: copied (not moved) as new ContactPerson records on the Customer.
invariants: At conversion: COPIED to Customer as ContactPerson records. Original stays on Prospect.

entities.name: ProspectProduct
entities.description: Pre-configured product/service for a Prospect. At conversion step 2 (DESTRUCTIVE): deletes existing CustomerProducts then creates new ones from ProspectProducts.
invariants: R038 — Conversion step 2 REPLACES CustomerProducts. Destructive. Must be intentional.

entities.name: ProspectUserRoleMapping
entities.description: Pre-configured user role assignments for a Prospect. These are NOT auto-activated at conversion (R040). They remain on Prospect as historical pre-configuration record. Absence of activation is the business rule.
invariants: R040 — NOT consumed at conversion. DO NOT implement auto-activation without explicit new business requirement.

entities.name: ProcessTask
entities.description: Work item owned by either a Prospect or a Customer. Polymorphic ownership via owner_type + owner_id (NOT FK-based). Template governs allowed owner_type. At conversion: tasks STAY ON PROSPECT (architect decision). Customer starts clean.
fields: ownerId (identifier — resolves to Prospect.id or Customer.id per ownerType)
invariants: R039 — NOT transferred at Prospect→Customer conversion. LEAVE ON PROSPECT.
owner_type must match the allowed_owner_types in the referenced ProcessTaskTemplate
owner_id must reference a valid entity of the declared owner_type (enforced in application layer — no DB FK)

entities.name: ProcessTaskTemplate
entities.description: Template that defines a class of work items. Constrains which owner_type is allowed for tasks created from this template.
invariants: ProcessTask.ownerType must be in template.allowedOwnerTypes

cross_aggregate_constraints.name: IDeliveryStatusWriter
cross_aggregate_constraints.description: All channels (SMS, Email, Voice, Eboks) that create DeliveryStatusEvent records MUST use IDeliveryStatusWriter from Messaging aggregate. Direct writes to the underlying table are prohibited (R036, SC002).

cross_aggregate_constraints.name: StatusProgressionGuard
cross_aggregate_constraints.description: Message.statusCode and DeliveryStatusEvent.imported may only move forward in rank. Applied at all write points in Messaging aggregate. Terminal statuses are immutable (R035, SC001).

cross_aggregate_constraints.name: ProfileGate
cross_aggregate_constraints.description: All Broadcast operations are scoped to a ProfileId. Role checks (ProfileRoleAssignment) applied at Broadcast create and activate. NOT enforced by DB — enforced by Messaging feature handlers.

cross_aggregate_constraints.name: AddressAdapterBoundary
cross_aggregate_constraints.description: Core domain (Messaging) only receives Kvhx strings from Address aggregate. Country-native data structures never cross the adapter boundary (R043, SC005).

cross_aggregate_constraints.name: ProspectConversionConstraint
cross_aggregate_constraints.description: Prospect→Customer conversion is two-step non-atomic (R038). ProcessTasks NOT transferred (R039). ProspectUserRoleMappings NOT activated (R040). These are hard constraints — do not add migration logic without new explicit business requirement.
